name: tutorial-fan-out-orchestration
title: Fan-out Orchestration
description: Parent workflow that fans a child workflow out to every item in a list, waits for a collection window, then gathers the replies. Demonstrates action spawn with forEach (${item.*}), the wait action, and ${steps.*} interpolation. Distilled from apps/team-pulse.
preamble:
## Domain Context
A coordinator launches one child workflow per reviewer to collect parallel input, waits for a fixed window, then aggregates whatever came back. This is the canonical fan-out shape: one parent run spawns N child runs and reconciles them.

triggerInput.name: question
triggerInput.description: Question sent to each reviewer's child workflow

triggerInput.name: waitMinutes
triggerInput.description: Minutes to wait for child replies before gathering

steps.name: Build batch
steps.description: Produce the list of items to fan out over. Real workflows would fetch this from an API; kept inline and tiny here on purpose.

steps.name: Fan out
steps.description: Spawn one collect-one child workflow per reviewer. forEach iterates the list; the single targets template reads ${item.*}.

steps.name: Await replies
steps.description: Wait a fixed window for child workflows to report back.

steps.name: Gather replies
steps.description: Read the spawned child handles and summarize how many replied. A production version fetches each instance's output via the platform API.
